item.name: Verify update creation when Auto Pay is false and a valid default payment method is provided.

exec: // Save the access token to environment variable
// Helper to check if a value is null, undefined, or blank
return value === null || value === undefined || (typeof value === 'string' && value.trim() === '');
// Helper to check if default payment method ID is valid (assuming UUID format)
// Test PUT update request status based on AutoPay and default_payment_method_id
pm.test("Validate update request status based on AutoPay and default payment method", function () {
console.log("Update rejected: auto_pay is null or blank.");
console.log("Update rejected: auto_pay is true but default_payment_method_id is null or missing.");
console.log("Update rejected: auto_pay is true but default_payment_method_id is invalid.");
console.log("Update successful: auto_pay is true with valid default payment method.");
console.log("Update rejected: auto_pay is false but default_payment_method_id is invalid.");
console.log("Update successful: auto_pay is false with valid default payment method.");

item.name: Verify account update when Auto Pay is false and an invalid default payment method is provided.

exec: // Save the access token to environment variable
// Helper to check if a value is null, undefined, or blank
return value === null || value === undefined || (typeof value === 'string' && value.trim() === '');
// Helper to check if default payment method ID is valid (assuming UUID format)
// Test PUT update request status based on AutoPay and default_payment_method_id
pm.test("Validate update request status based on AutoPay and default payment method", function () {
console.log("Update rejected: auto_pay is null or blank.");
console.log("Update rejected: auto_pay is true but default_payment_method_id is null or missing.");
console.log("Update rejected: auto_pay is true but default_payment_method_id is invalid.");
console.log("Update successful: auto_pay is true with valid default payment method.");
console.log("Update rejected: auto_pay is false but default_payment_method_id is invalid.");
console.log("Update successful: auto_pay is false with valid default payment method.");
console.log("Update successful: auto_pay is false with no default payment method.");
// Send GET request to verify AutoPay and default_payment_method_id update status
pm.test("Verify AutoPay and default_payment_method_id update status", function () {
console.log(`PASS: auto_pay updated to '${actualAutoPay}', default_payment_method_id updated to '${actualDefaultPmId}'.`);
console.error(`FAIL: Mismatch detected. Expected auto_pay: '${updatedAutoPay}', got: '${actualAutoPay}'. Expected default_payment_method_id: '${updatedDefaultPmId}', got: '${actualDefaultPmId}'.`);
pm.expect.fail("AutoPay or default payment method ID mismatch between update and GET response.");

pm.expect.fail("Name mismatch between update request and GET response.");

pm.test("Update request validation for bill_to mobile_phone", function () {
// Expect validation error for null/blank mobile_phone
// Expect validation error for invalid mobile_phone format
// Send GET request to verify mobile_phone update status
pm.test("Verify bill_to mobile_phone update status", function () {

console.error(`FAIL: mobile_phone was incorrectly updated to '${actualMobilePhone}'.`);
pm.expect.fail(`mobile_phone should not be updated to null/blank/invalid value.`);

// For valid mobile_phone, verify it was updated correctly

exec: // Save the access token to environment variable
// Parse PUT request body to get sold_to first_name and last_name
// Helper function to check if a value is null, undefined, or blank
return value === null || value === undefined || (typeof value === 'string' && value.trim() === '');
// Test PUT update request validation for mandatory sold_to name fields
pm.test("Update request validation for sold_to first_name and last_name", function () {
// Send GET request to verify sold_to first_name and last_name update status
pm.test("Verify sold_to first_name and last_name update status", function () {
// If mandatory fields were missing in update, verify they were NOT updated to blank/null
console.error(`FAIL: sold_to first_name and/or last_name were incorrectly updated to blank/null.`);
pm.expect.fail("Mandatory sold_to name fields should not be updated to blank or null.");
console.log(`PASS: sold_to first_name and last_name remain unchanged; update with missing mandatory fields was rejected.`);
// If both fields were provided, verify they were updated correctly
console.log(`PASS: sold_to first_name and last_name updated successfully.`);
console.error(`FAIL: sold_to name mismatch. Expected first_name: '${updatedFirstName}', got: '${actualFirstName}'; Expected last_name: '${updatedLastName}', got: '${actualLastName}'.`);
pm.expect.fail("Mismatch between update request and GET response for sold_to name fields.");
